title: taraxacum officinale subsp. aquilonare
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. aquilonare, also known as the Canadian dandelion, is a subspecies of the common dandelion, a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the sunflower family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic, helping to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections and other conditions. The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale subsp. aquilonare include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve liver function, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its roots and leaves can be used in herbal preparations, such as capsules or dried powder, to promote overall well-being.
